artifact.note: hash-anchored to the on-disk macOS GGUF — NOTE this sha256 differs from the Windows manifest's (3f87a880027e7b9ea8e0da9e4009584336f352af444a0e6e5c20721ac4c7ffd1): a distinct on-disk Q8_0 file, same model/quant.

chat_template_excerpt:
{{- bos_token }} ... system branch: {%- if messages[0]['role']=='system' %} -> "<|start_header_id|>system<|end_header_id|>

"+system_message+"<|eot_id|>"; if tools: "Environment: ipython"; loop: "<|start_header_id|>"+role+"<|end_header_id|>

"+content|trim+"<|eot_id|>"; tool branch: {%- elif 'tool_calls' in message %} -> {"name":..,"parameters":..}; {%- elif role=="tool" or "ipython" %} -> "<|start_header_id|>ipython<|end_header_id|>"; {%- if add_generation_prompt %} -> assistant header

claimed_envelope.model_evidence: Decoder/API-side n>1 independent generations; model-agnostic for any causal LM (OpenAI-shape contract).
stream_options.include_usage terminal usage chunk is API-shape, model-agnostic (already shipped in Camelid main lane).

claimed_envelope.capability: max_tokens, stop sequences, EOS stop behavior
claimed_envelope.model_evidence: EOS stop token declared: tokenizer.ggml.eos_token_id=128009 (<|eot_id|>); max_tokens/stop are API contract.
top_p/top_k/min_p/temperature/penalties/logit_bias are sampler invariants, model-agnostic for any causal LM.

claimed_envelope.capability: fixed seed reproduces token-for-token across runs
claimed_envelope.model_evidence: Fixed-seed token-for-token reproducibility is a sampler/RNG invariant independent of weights.
logprobs/top_logprobs at temp=0 derive deterministically from the logits any causal LM emits.

claimed_envelope.capability: system role + multi-turn template fidelity (per THIS template)
claimed_envelope.model_evidence: VERIFIED: explicit system extraction "{%- if messages[0]['role'] == 'system' %}" sets system_message, then unconditional "<|start_header_id|>system<|end_header_id|>\n\n"+system_message+"<|eot_id|>" block; multi-turn loop "{%- for message in messages %}" emitting "<|start_header_id|>"+role+"<|end_header_id|>" per user/assistant turn with <|eot_id|>.

claimed_envelope.capability: native tool/function calling — REQUIRES tool-call branch or tool/ipython control tokens in THIS model template
claimed_envelope.model_evidence: VERIFIED specific template branch "{%- elif 'tool_calls' in message %}" emitting {"name": tool_call.name, "parameters": tool_call.arguments|tojson} under assistant header; plus tools-gated "Environment: ipython" line, tools_in_user_message JSON-function guidance block, and tool-response role header "<|start_header_id|>ipython<|end_header_id|>" (role==tool/ipython branch). Template-expressed -> capable.

claimed_envelope.capability: JSON mode + GBNF grammar-constrained decode (decoder-side, model-agnostic)
claimed_envelope.model_evidence: JSON mode + GBNF grammar-constrained decode is decoder-side and model-agnostic for any causal LM.

claimed_envelope.capability: full TRAINED context length, exact value from metadata
claimed_envelope.model_evidence: RE-READ from metadata: llama.context_length=131072 (exact trained/extended length).
claimed_envelope.macos_status_note: host-limited on 16GB Metal (trained 131072 context KV beyond safe RAM); not a model limit.

claimed_envelope.capability: RoPE scaling/extension — REQUIRES a rope scaling type/factor declared in metadata
claimed_envelope.model_evidence: RE-VERIFIED: full metadata key list (30 keys) contains NO llama.rope.scaling.type/.factor/.original_context_length; only llama.rope.freq_base=500000.0 and llama.rope.dimension_count=64. A rope_freqs.weight tensor exists (llama3 scaling baked into weights) but there is NO metadata-declared scaling config to validate -> incapable (stricter verdict, correct n/a).
claimed_envelope.synthesis_correction: CORRECTED in synthesis (anti-underclaim §0/§4.6): the GGUF carries a rope_freqs.weight tensor = llama3 rope scaling baked into weights (no metadata rope.scaling.* key, but the scaling is real and reaches 131072); Camelid applies the llama3 path (rope.rs:507). Validate parity at extended positions vs llama.cpp (class D).

claimed_envelope.capability: prompt/completion/total token accounting + optional timing fields
claimed_envelope.model_evidence: prompt/completion/total token accounting + timing fields are API-shape, model-agnostic.

claimed_envelope.capability: (SECONDARY) loadability across additional quants beyond Q8_0
claimed_envelope.model_evidence: general.file_type=7 (Q8_0); llama arch loads other quants too — widens load matrix, not behavioral envelope (SECONDARY).
claimed_envelope.macos_status_note: secondary axis; not exercised on Metal in Bucket A.

overclaim_flags: ARCH CORRECTION: removed "ipython" from arch.special_tokens — it is NOT a declared vocab control token, only a chat-template header ROLE string used as <|start_header_id|>ipython<|end_header_id|>. Remaining special_tokens are the 5 genuinely confirmed control tokens (bos 128000, eot 128009, <|start_header_id|>, <|end_header_id|>, finetune_right_pad 128004).

context.rope_scaling KEPT incapable (oracle_class D): re-read all 30 metadata keys — no llama.rope.scaling.* keys present (only rope.freq_base + rope.dimension_count). rope_freqs.weight tensor exists but carries no metadata-declared scaling type/factor to validate; stricter verdict retained.

tools.function_calling KEPT capable (oracle_class B): adversarially confirmed the SPECIFIC template branch '{%- elif "tool_calls" in message %}' emitting {"name","parameters"} JSON plus tools-gated 'Environment: ipython' and ipython tool-role header — capability is template-expressed, so capable is justified, not an overclaim.

chat.system_multiturn KEPT capable (oracle_class D): confirmed explicit '{%- if messages[0]["role"] == "system" %}' system extraction + unconditional system header block + per-turn message loop with role headers and <|eot_id|>.
context.full_length KEPT capable (oracle_class D): re-read llama.context_length=131072 directly from metadata — exact trained length confirmed.
eos_token_ids KEPT as [128009]: metadata declares only tokenizer.ggml.eos_token_id=128009; did NOT add 128001/<|end_of_text|> since it is not declared as an EOS in this GGUF (strict reading).
phase0_notes: Adversarial Phase 0 re-verification of llama-3.2-1b-instruct-q8_0 against fresh `camelid inspect` metadata (30 keys, 147 tensors). No verdict flips warranted: every 'capable' cap points to a specific template branch, control token, or metadata key, and context.rope_scaling 'incapable' is correct (no rope.scaling.* metadata despite the rope_freqs.weight tensor). One arch fix: dropped the bogus 'ipython' special-token entry (it is a header role, not a vocab control token). Confirmed exact values: architecture=llama, bos=128000, eos=128009 (sole declared), pad=128004, context_length=131072, rope.freq_base=500000.0. Envelope is sound after corrections.
phase2_macos_notes: WIN2METAL Phase 2 (Bucket A) on Apple M4 16GB, GPU-resident Metal decode. Validation only — frozen base binary 28f224b, no src changes. Behavioral/contract/sampler caps re-run e2e on Metal (no Metal-specific regression); class-D parity caps (chat.system_multiturn, context.rope_scaling) byte-exact vs llama.cpp Metal. context.full_length stays host-limited (trained 131072 KV beyond safe RAM on 16GB). load.quant_breadth not exercised (secondary). logprobs watch item RESOLVED PASS (full-vocab LogitsStage forced under the resident Metal decode, not the GPU-sample shortcut).
